---
title: "Lecture 11 Notes: Control II - Operational-Space Control"
---

## Lecture slide

- [Lecture 11 slides](../slides/11-control-2-operational-space-control.qmd)

## Key ideas

- Operational-space control directly regulates end-effector motion/force.
- Jacobians map between joint space and task space.
- Redundant robots can execute secondary objectives in null space.

## Core relation

\[
\dot{x} = J(q)\dot{q}, \quad \tau = J^T F + N(q)\tau_{null}
\]

## Review questions

1. Why is task-space control useful for manipulation tasks?
2. What is null-space motion and when is it beneficial?
3. How do joint limits change operational-space design?
